old_value: {"id":"Forge-d89","title":"Fix daemon.IsRunning() on Windows (Signal(0) not supported)","description":"daemon.IsRunning() calls proc.Signal(syscall.Signal(0)) to check process liveness. On Windows this is not supported and always returns an error, so IsRunning() always returns false. Fix by using ipc.SocketExists() as the liveness check on Windows, since the named pipe is destroyed when the process dies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T11:13:43Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T11:13:43Z"}

Create the command file to open the Bubbletea TUI dashboard.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T11:13:53Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T11:13:53Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Research needed before implementing: (1) What exit code and/or stderr message does 'claude' emit specifically on rate limit exhaustion vs other failures — this is needed to distinguish rate limit from real errors in smith.Spawn. (2) Whether 'gemini' CLI and 'gh copilot suggest' accept equivalent flags to claude's '-p', '--output-format stream-json', '--verbose', '--dangerously-skip-permissions' — or whether the provider layer needs to translate/adapt arguments and parse output differently per provider."}
old_value: {"id":"Forge-7ci","title":"Fallback to Gemini then Copilot CLI when Claude rate limit hit","description":"Forge currently only supports Claude Code CLI ('claude'). When the Pro plan rate limit is exhausted, smiths fail. Implement provider fallback: 1) detect rate limit exit (claude exits with specific code or stderr message), 2) retry with Gemini CLI if available, 3) retry with GitHub Copilot CLI if Gemini unavailable. Config should allow specifying provider order and CLI command names. The smith.Spawn interface needs to be provider-agnostic or have a provider selection layer above it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T12:35:05Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T12:35:05Z"}

{"id":"Forge-7ci","title":"Fallback to Gemini then Copilot CLI when Claude rate limit hit","description":"Forge currently only supports Claude Code CLI ('claude'). When the Pro plan rate limit is exhausted, smiths fail. Implement provider fallback: 1) detect rate limit exit (claude exits with specific code or stderr message), 2) retry with Gemini CLI if available, 3) retry with GitHub Copilot CLI if Gemini unavailable. Config should allow specifying provider order and CLI command names. The smith.Spawn interface needs to be provider-agnostic or have a provider selection layer above it.","notes":"Research needed before implementing: (1) What exit code and/or stderr message does 'claude' emit specifically on rate limit exhaustion vs other failures — this is needed to distinguish rate limit from real errors in smith.Spawn. (2) Whether 'gemini' CLI and 'gh copilot suggest' accept equivalent flags to claude's '-p', '--output-format stream-json', '--verbose', '--dangerously-skip-permissions' — or whether the provider layer needs to translate/adapt arguments and parse output differently per provider.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T12:35:05Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T12:35:51Z"}

old_value: {"id":"Forge-f4q","title":"Orphan cleanup should reset bead status to open","description":"When the daemon starts and cleans up orphaned worker records (dead processes), it updates the DB worker status but does not reset the corresponding bead in bd. The bead stays in_progress forever and bd ready never returns it. The orphan cleanup in shutdown.Manager should call 'bd update \u003cbeadID\u003e --status=open' for each orphaned worker it cleans up.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T12:33:25Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T12:33:25Z"}

If neither CLI exposes quota directly, at minimum capture and display the reset time from Claude's rate_limit_event payload (it already arrives in the stream).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:57Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:57Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Neither Claude nor Gemini CLIs expose a standalone quota-check command.

Claude CLI (confirmed from docs at code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference):
- No "claude --usage", "claude usage", or "claude api usage" subcommand exists.
- The only relevant command is "claude auth status" (shows auth status as JSON, not quota).
- Quota/rate-limit info arrives exclusively via the streaming rate_limit_event, already captured
  in smith.go as RateLimitInfo{Status string, ...}.
- The rate_limit_event payload includes a "reset_at" RFC3339 timestamp telling us when the
  limit resets. This is NOT yet stored in RateLimitInfo (only Status is kept).
- Implementation: add ResetAt string to RateLimitInfo, populate from rate_limit_info.reset_at
  in readStreamJSON, surface in forge status output and hearth TUI sidebar.

Gemini CLI (confirmed from gemini-cli source and docs/resources/quota-and-pricing.md):
- No "gemini --quota", "gemini stats", or similar non-interactive quota command.
- Quota data is only available inside an active interactive session via the "/stats model"
  slash command (which calls config.getQuotaRemaining/getQuotaLimit/getQuotaResetTime).
- Quota remaining/limit/resetTime come from API response headers and are tracked in Config
  internally; there is no way to query them outside a live session.
- When Gemini hits a rate limit, the error text contains the reset time; we already detect
  this in daemon.go (rate_limit_event detection). We can parse "retry after N seconds" or
  "reset at <time>" from the error output to get a Gemini-side reset time.
- The result-event stats field (total_tokens, duration_ms etc.) does NOT include quota info.

Scoped implementation:
1. Add ResetAt string field to RateLimitInfo in smith.go.
2. Populate ResetAt from rate_limit_info.reset_at in readStreamJSON (Claude path).
3. For Gemini: attempt to parse "retry after N seconds" or RFC3339 time from ErrorOutput
   when IsRateLimited is set; store as computed reset time.
4. Persist last-seen RateLimitInfo per provider in daemon state (in-memory or state.db).
5. Include provider quota/reset info in "forge status" JSON output.
6. Show in hearth TUI: a per-provider "rate limited until <time>" indicator when relevant.","status":"open"}

{"id":"Forge-2ur","title":"Configurable per-anvil auto-dispatch behavior","description":"Currently Forge dispatches ALL ready beads from 'bd ready --json' automatically. Add configurable control over which beads get auto-dispatched, on a per-anvil basis.

New per-anvil config fields (in AnvilConfig in internal/config/config.go):
  auto_dispatch: all | tagged | priority | off
    - all: current behavior, dispatch everything ready (default)
    - tagged: only dispatch beads whose 'tags' field contains auto_dispatch_tag
    - priority: only dispatch beads at or above auto_dispatch_min_priority
    - off: never auto-dispatch; beads only run via 'forge queue run <id>'
  auto_dispatch_tag: string  (used when auto_dispatch: tagged, e.g. 'forge-auto')
  auto_dispatch_min_priority: int  (used when auto_dispatch: priority, e.g. 1 means P0+P1 only)

Implementation notes:
- Add Tags []string json:'tags' to poller.Bead (bd ready JSON may include a tags array; if absent, tags-mode falls through to dispatch nothing).
- Add the new fields to AnvilConfig with defaults (auto_dispatch defaults to 'all').
- Apply the filter after polling in poller.Poll or in daemon.go before dispatching.
- Validate the config: if auto_dispatch=tagged, auto_dispatch_tag must be non-empty; if auto_dispatch=priority, min_priority must be 0-4.
- Update forge.yaml sample/docs with the new knobs.
- The 'off' mode is useful for repos where Forge should observe beads but never touch them without explicit human invocation.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:17:13Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:17:13Z"}

{"id":"Forge-l3z","title":"Add GitHub Actions CI workflow","description":"Add a GitHub Actions CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml. The workflow should run on push and pull_request to main.

Jobs (all on ubuntu-latest, Go version from go.mod):
1. build - go build ./...
2. vet - go vet ./...
3. test - go test ./... (once Forge-tqb adds tests; initially just verifies compilation)
4. golangci-lint (optional, use golangci/golangci-lint-action) - catches formatting and common issues

Workflow triggers:
  on:
    push:
      branches: [main]
    pull_request:
      branches: [main]

Key requirements:
- Use actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-go@v5 with cache: true
- Store Go cache between runs (setup-go handles this automatically with cache: true)
- Fail fast on build/vet before running tests
- The workflow filename should be ci.yml; the workflow name "CI"
- Add a status badge to README.md once it is green

Note: There is no Makefile currently. The CI should use plain go commands, not make targets.
No release or publish step needed here - this is only for PR validation and branch protection.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:26:55Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:26:55Z"}

{"id":"Forge-tqb","title":"Add unit tests for core packages","description":"Add unit tests for Forge's core packages. The codebase currently has no test files.

Priority packages to cover (in order):
1. internal/config - config loading, validation, defaults, poll_interval parsing, env var override
2. internal/poller - mock bd output, priority sorting, per-anvil merge, error handling
3. internal/smith - readStreamJSON: parse Claude/Gemini stream-json events, rate_limit_event, result event stats
4. internal/provider - BuildArgs output for each provider, Defaults() ordering
5. internal/ghpr - PRStatus methods (IsMerged, NeedsChanges, CIsPassing, HasApproval)
6. internal/bellows - snapshot diff logic: that events fire on transitions, not on repeat state

Test conventions:
- Use t.TempDir() for any filesystem work
- Use table-driven tests ([]struct{name, input, want}) throughout
- Mock external processes (bd, gh, claude, gemini) by swapping the exec.Command function or using a test binary approach
- No integration tests that require live network/credentials
- Place tests in the same package (_test suffix files) or a separate _test package where internal access is needed
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:26:22Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:26:22Z"}

{"id":"Forge-7ug","title":"Fix bellows+reviewfix: wrong trigger, wrong API, no thread resolution","description":"Bellows + reviewfix have multiple interconnected bugs that prevent automatic PR fix triggering. PR #2 (Forge-f4q) demonstrates all of them.

## Bug 1 — Bellows trigger fires on wrong condition

bellows.go: NeedsChanges() only returns true for review state "CHANGES_REQUESTED".
Copilot (and many human reviewers) submit reviews as "COMMENTED" state with inline
thread suggestions. These never trigger EventReviewChanges.

Fix: Add a new prSnapshot field `HasUnresolvedThreads bool`. In checkPR, after
calling ghpr.CheckStatus, additionally call a new ghpr.FetchUnresolvedThreadCount
that uses the GraphQL reviewThreads query (see Bug 2). If unresolved thread count
transitions from 0 to >0 (or PR is newly seen with unresolved threads), emit
EventReviewChanges so reviewfix is triggered.

ghpr.CheckStatus should also be extended to include unresolved thread count alongside
the existing reviews/CI data.

## Bug 2 — fetchReviewComments uses wrong API; misses inline threads and isResolved

reviewfix.go fetchReviewComments: uses `gh pr view --json reviews,comments`.
The `comments` field in that API is PR-level general comments, NOT inline review
thread comments (which live in `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{prNumber}/comments`
or preferably via GraphQL reviewThreads). There is no isResolved field in the REST
API — only GraphQL exposes it.

Fix: Replace fetchReviewComments with a GraphQL-based implementation:

  gh api graphql -f query='
  query($owner:String!, $repo:String!, $pr:Int!) {
    repository(owner:$owner, name:$repo) {
      pullRequest(number:$pr) {
        reviewThreads(first:100) {
          nodes {
            id
            isResolved
            comments(first:1) {
              nodes { path line body author { login } }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }' -f owner=OWNER -f repo=REPO -F pr=NUMBER

Filter nodes where isResolved: false. Map each to ReviewComment{ThreadID: node.id, ...}.
The owner/repo must be extracted from `git remote get-url origin` in the worktree.

Also fetch PR-level review body comments (non-thread) via:
  gh pr view {number} --comments
and include those that are not purely informational (heuristic: body length > 20 chars,
not an approval message).

## Bug 3 — No thread resolution after fix

After Smith successfully applies fixes and pushes, reviewfix returns without calling
the resolveReviewThread GraphQL mutation for the addressed threads.

Fix: After Smith exits with code 0, for each comment whose ThreadID is non-empty,
call:

  gh api graphql -f query='
  mutation($threadId:ID!) {
    resolveReviewThread(input:{threadId:$threadId}) {
      thread { isResolved }
    }
  }' -f threadId="{thread.id}"

Add a resolveThread(ctx, worktreePath, threadID string) error helper in reviewfix.go.
Log success/failure per thread but do not fail the overall FixResult if resolution
calls fail (thread resolution is best-effort).

## Bug 4 — CI failure event not wired to a fix worker

When bellows emits EventCIFailed, check daemon.go to confirm whether a handler
actually spawns a Smith worker to fix it. If not (likely), add a ci_fix handler
analogous to the review_fix handler: spawn Smith with a prompt listing the failing
CI checks (from gh pr checks output) and instruct it to fix build/test errors.

## Implementation order

1. Fix fetchReviewComments (Bug 2) — foundational for everything else
2. Add resolveThread after fix (Bug 3)
3. Fix bellows trigger to use unresolved thread count (Bug 1)
4. Wire EventCIFailed to a ci fix smith in daemon.go (Bug 4)

## GraphQL mutation reference (from Heimdall pr-fix skill)

Resolve thread:
  gh api graphql -f query='mutation($threadId:ID!) { resolveReviewThread(input:{threadId:$threadId}) { thread { isResolved } } }' -f threadId="THREAD_NODE_ID"

Query threads:
  gh api graphql -f query='query($owner:String!, $repo:String!, $pr:Int!) { repository(owner:$owner,name:$repo) { pullRequest(number:$pr) { reviewThreads(first:100) { nodes { id isResolved comments(first:1) { nodes { path line body author { login } } } } } } } }' -f owner=OWNER -f repo=REPO -F pr=NUMBER
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:31:04Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:31:04Z"}

new_value: Implemented Bugs 1-4 and verified with build and basic tests.

{"id":"Forge-4ei","title":"Configurable per-anvil auto-dispatch behavior","description":"Currently Forge dispatches ALL ready beads from 'bd ready --json' automatically. Add configurable control over which beads get auto-dispatched, on a per-anvil basis.

New per-anvil config fields (in AnvilConfig in internal/config/config.go):
  auto_dispatch: all | tagged | priority | off
    - all: current behavior, dispatch everything ready (default)
    - tagged: only dispatch beads whose 'tags' field contains auto_dispatch_tag
    - priority: only dispatch beads at or above auto_dispatch_min_priority
    - off: never auto-dispatch; beads only run via 'forge queue run <id>'
  auto_dispatch_tag: string  (used when auto_dispatch: tagged, e.g. 'forge-auto')
  auto_dispatch_min_priority: int  (used when auto_dispatch: priority, e.g. 1 means P0+P1 only)

Implementation notes:
- Add Tags []string json:'tags' to poller.Bead (bd ready JSON may include a tags array; if absent, tags-mode falls through to dispatch nothing).
- Add the new fields to AnvilConfig with defaults (auto_dispatch defaults to 'all').
- Apply the filter after polling in poller.Poll or in daemon.go before dispatching.
- Validate the config: if auto_dispatch=tagged, auto_dispatch_tag must be non-empty; if auto_dispatch=priority, min_priority must be 0-4.
- Update forge.yaml sample/docs with the new knobs.
- The 'off' mode is useful for repos where Forge should observe beads but never touch them without explicit human invocation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:16:54Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:16:54Z"}

new_value: Implemented per-anvil auto-dispatch and added 'forge queue run <id>' for manual execution

{"id":"Forge-ebr","title":"Add event logging to warden, temper, and bellows","description":"Currently the warden, temper, and bellows components do not emit any structured events to the state DB event log. Add db.LogEvent calls at key lifecycle points: warden should log when it starts a review (EventInfo with bead ID) and when it finishes (EventInfo with verdict + summary). Temper should log when it starts checks, and when it passes or fails. Bellows should log when it starts monitoring, finds CI failures, or posts review fix comments. Follow the existing pattern in daemon.go and pipeline.go.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:29Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:29Z"}

old_value: {"id":"Forge-ibz","title":"Hearth Workers view: show active component per worker (smith/temper/warden/bellows)","description":"In Hearth Workers panel each row shows a bead/worker entry but does not indicate which Forge component is currently active for it (Smith writing code, Temper running checks, Bellows monitoring, Warden reviewing). Fix: add a component indicator column or tag to each worker row. The worker state in the state DB tracks status (running, pending, done, failed) but not the active sub-component. Options: (1) Store the active component name in the workers table (e.g. a new phase column: smith|temper|warden|bellows|idle) and update it at each pipeline phase transition in pipeline.go and bellows.go. (2) Alternatively derive it from the event log: the most recent event for that bead ID implies the phase. Display in the Workers panel as a short tag e.g. [smith] [temper] [warden] alongside the bead ID and status. Color coding: smith=yellow (writing), temper=cyan (checking), warden=magenta (reviewing), bellows=blue (monitoring).\r\n","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:38:06Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:38:06Z"}

new_value: Implemented active component display in Hearth Workers panel.

old_value: {"id":"Forge-olo","title":"Parse and log Gemini stats from result event","description":"Gemini's stream-json result event does not have a 'total_cost_usd' field but does include a 'stats' object: {total_tokens, input_tokens, output_tokens, cached, input, duration_ms, tool_calls}. Currently readStreamJSON only reads TotalCostUSD so Gemini runs always show cost=0 and token counts=0. Fix: add a Stats field to StreamEvent matching Gemini's stats shape, populate CostUSD (Gemini has no cost but we can estimate from token counts if desired, or just leave 0), populate TokensIn/TokensOut from stats.input_tokens/output_tokens, and log the full stats summary (tokens, cache hits, tool_calls, duration_ms) to the smith log file and to an EventInfo so it appears in forge history output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:51Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:06:51Z"}

{"id":"Forge-wld","title":"Hearth event log: show full message text instead of truncating","description":"In the Hearth TUI, the Events panel truncates event message text to a single short
line. This forces the user to open log files manually to see what actually happened.

Fix: Increase the visible text per event row substantially. The Events panel should
show enough of the message that common cases (e.g. warden verdict, Smith summary,
CI failure reason, error text) are readable in-place.

Implementation notes:
- internal/hearth/ contains the Bubbletea TUI
- The Events panel renders rows from the state DB event log (db.ListEvents or similar)
- Increase the per-row character limit from whatever it currently is to at least 200
  characters (or ideally wrap onto a second line if the panel height allows)
- If the panel uses a fixed column width, derive the wrap width dynamically from
  the actual panel width (m.width or equivalent) so it adapts to terminal size
- For the event body/message field specifically: prefer wrapping over hard truncation
  so the user can see the full content by scrolling within the panel
- If a viewport or list component is used, ensure its height is sufficient to show
  2-3 lines per event when text wraps
- The timestamp + event type prefix can stay compact; only the message body should wrap

- Use actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-go@v5 with cache: true
- Store Go cache between runs (setup-go handles this automatically with cache: true)
- Fail fast on build/vet before running tests
- The workflow filename should be ci.yml; the workflow name "CI"
- Add a status badge to README.md once it is green

Note: There is no Makefile currently. The CI should use plain go commands, not make targets.
No release or publish step needed here - this is only for PR validation and branch protection.

{"id":"Forge-cvm","title":"Add GitHub Actions CI workflow","description":"Add a GitHub Actions CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml. The workflow should run on push and pull_request to main.

Jobs (all on ubuntu-latest, Go version from go.mod):
1. build - go build ./...
2. vet - go vet ./...
3. test - go test ./... (once Forge-tqb adds tests; initially just verifies compilation)
4. golangci-lint (optional, use golangci/golangci-lint-action) - catches formatting and common issues

Workflow triggers:
  on:
    push:
      branches: [main]
    pull_request:
      branches: [main]

Key requirements:
- Use actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-go@v5 with cache: true
- Store Go cache between runs (setup-go handles this automatically with cache: true)
- Fail fast on build/vet before running tests
- The workflow filename should be ci.yml; the workflow name "CI"
- Add a status badge to README.md once it is green

Note: There is no Makefile currently. The CI should use plain go commands, not make targets.
No release or publish step needed here - this is only for PR validation and branch protection.

{"id":"Forge-rz8","title":"Hearth: clamp panel widths and heights to prevent negative dimensions on narrow terminals","description":"PR #6 (line 203, hearth.go) and PR #14 (lines 226, 402, hearth.go) both flag that width/height math can go negative or overflow on small terminals.

Specific issues:
- availableHeight can be < minTop+minBottom; the current code does not degrade gracefully and can push content off-screen
- remainingWidth (m.width - 4) can go negative for very narrow terminals, yielding negative panel widths
- interiorWidth is hard-clamped to 20 even when panelWidth-4 is smaller, so wrapped lines can exceed the panel

Fix: clamp availableHeight >= 0 first, then proportionally degrade panel heights when the terminal cannot satisfy both minimums; ensure all width values are max(0, computed) before use; keep interiorWidth <= panelWidth-4 (minimum 1, not 20).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:35:48Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:35:48Z"}

{"id":"Forge-63p","title":"Hearth: fix wordWrap to preserve newlines and use unicode-aware width","description":"PR #6 (hearth.go lines 566-583, 615) flags two correctness issues in wordWrap:

1. wordWrap calls strings.Fields which splits on all whitespace including newlines, collapsing repeated spaces and discarding intentional line breaks. Event log messages may contain formatted output, stack traces, or indented text that should be preserved. Fix: split on actual newlines first (wrap per paragraph), then word-wrap each paragraph independently.

2. Width decisions use len(word) (byte count) rather than display width, so Unicode/multibyte characters wrap at wrong positions. Fix: use lipgloss.Width or a runewidth helper for display-width calculations.

3. strings.TrimSpace(pStr) on every word in renderEventLines removes leading indentation from continuation lines, which corrupts formatted event output. Remove the TrimSpace or only apply it to detect blank lines.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:04Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:04Z"}

{"id":"Forge-0rx","title":"Config: improve auto_dispatch_min_priority validation error message to clarify 0=critical-only","description":"PR #14 (config.go line 202): the validation was updated to allow 0 as a valid value for auto_dispatch_min_priority when auto_dispatch is 'priority', but the error message no longer explains what 0 means operationally.\n\nAdd a hint in the error message, e.g.: 'auto_dispatch_min_priority must be 0-4 (0 = critical-only) when auto_dispatch is priority' so users can make an informed choice without reading source code.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:32Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:32Z"}

{"id":"Forge-22h","title":"Hearth: eliminate redundant renderAllEventLines calls in scrollDown and renderEventLines","description":"PR #6 and PR #14 both flag duplicate rendering work in the event panel:

1. scrollDown() calls renderAllEventLines() just to compute len(allLines) for scroll bounds. Since View()/renderEvents() also calls renderAllEventLines(), every scroll keypress triggers the full wrap/render pass twice.

2. renderAllEventLines() calls renderEventLines() twice per event: once to determine selection mapping and once to append lines. Each call does wrapping and string allocation.

Fix: cache the flattened rendered-line slice for the current terminal width and invalidate on width change or new events. Alternatively, add a lightweight lineCount(event, width) helper that avoids string allocation so scrollDown can get the count without a full render.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:19Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:19Z"}

{"id":"Forge-ojk","title":"Make review fix max attempts configurable","description":"In internal/reviewfix/reviewfix.go line 26, MaxAttempts is hardcoded to 2. A PR with 10+ review comments from Copilot can easily require more than 2 fix passes to resolve all issues. This makes it easier for new developers and AI agents to understand and configure Forge correctly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:00:35Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:00:35Z"}

{"id":"Forge-ruv","title":"Forge: detect and recover orphaned in-progress beads on startup","description":"On daemon startup (and periodically), beads with status=in_progress but no active worker and no open PR may be orphaned (interrupted mid-session). The daemon should identify these and either reset them to open (so they can be retried) or log them for operator review. Key constraints: only touch beads belonging to THIS forge config's anvils (never touch beads from other anvil configs), and never reset a bead that has an active worker or open PR.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:25:15Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:25:15Z"}

{"id":"Forge-c9z","title":"Create docs/ folder with Forge configuration reference","description":"Forge has no documentation beyond CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/README.md. This makes it easier for new developers and AI agents to understand and configure Forge correctly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:00:35Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:22:54Z"}

{"id":"Forge-fv5","title":"Hearth: selecting a worker shows live log output in activity panel","description":"The live activity panel in Hearth currently shows nothing. It should be interactive: when the user selects a worker in the worker list (tab 2), the activity panel on the right should display streaming log output from that worker's session log file (in .forge-logs/ under the worktree). The panel should tail the file and update as new lines are written. This makes it possible to see what Smith is doing in real time without leaving the TUI.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:57Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:39Z"}

{"id":"Forge-r3p","title":"Add tests for lifecycle.Manager state machine","description":"lifecycle.Manager is the central dispatch hub (CI fix, review fix, bead close, conflict detection) but has zero tests. Add table-driven tests covering: EventCIFailed dispatch, max CI attempts exhaustion, EventReviewChanges, EventPRMerged closes bead, EventPRConflicting logging, and concurrent HandleEvent calls (race detector).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:22:41Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:54Z"}

{"id":"Forge-3fl","title":"Hearth: replace hard-coded event fetch limit 100 with a named constant","description":"PR #6 (data.go line 182): the event fetch in FetchEvents uses a hard-coded literal 100. With wrapping, long messages now consume multiple display lines, so the effective visible event count is lower than the limit. Define a named constant (e.g., eventFetchLimit = 100) in data.go or derive the value from the panel height so the intent is clear and the value is easy to adjust.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:36:45Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-05T21:51:45Z"}

{"id":"Forge-h7x","title":"Hearth: add Needs Human Attention panel for exhausted and stuck PRs","description":"When lifecycle_exhausted or all-providers-rate-limited events occur there is no dedicated UI surface showing what needs attention. Add a panel (or section) in Hearth that: (1) lists beads with needs_human=1 or stuck in_progress with no active worker, (2) shows the reason (exhausted CI fixes, exhausted review fixes, rate limited, collateral CI failure in unrelated package), (3) provides operator actions like reset-to-open or mark-resolved. For collateral CI failure case: when bellows fires EventCIFailed for a test unrelated to the PR changes, the fix agent fails and needs_human=1 is set — the panel should note this and suggest a rebase.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:56:37Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:56:37Z"}

{"id":"Forge-pgi","title":"Hearth: fix dual panel selection when switching panels","description":"When switching between panels in Hearth TUI, both the Workers panel and the Live Activity panel appear selected simultaneously. Focus/active state should be exclusive — only one panel selected at a time. Occurs when tab-switching between the worker list (tab 2) and the activity panel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:55:04Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:55:04Z"}

{"id":"Forge-rpc","title":"Pipeline: release bead back to open when all providers are rate limited","description":"When all providers are rate limited, pipeline.Run() fails with an 'All providers rate limited' error. The bead stays in_progress with no active worker and no way to recover without human intervention. Fix: detect this specific error in pipeline.Run() and automatically reset the bead status back to open via bd, then wait a configurable backoff period before the poller retries. Without this fix every rate-limit event leaves orphaned in_progress beads that need manual cleanup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:55:27Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:55:27Z"}

{"id":"Forge-bj3","title":"Reviewfix: auto-request Copilot re-review after pushing fix commit","description":"After reviewfix.Run() pushes a fix commit, GitHub Copilot does not automatically re-review the PR. The fix loop stalls because bellows never sees another EventReviewChanges trigger. Forge should call 'gh pr review-request \u003cpr\u003e --reviewer Copilot' (or equivalent GitHub API) after pushing review fixes so the review cycle continues. Also needs documentation in docs/ covering the reviewfix loop and re-review mechanism. Dependent on Forge-c9z (docs folder) being complete.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:57:03Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:57:03Z"}

{"id":"Forge-13q","title":"Pipeline/Warden: VerdictReject (no-diff) leaves bead stuck in_progress","description":"When warden returns VerdictReject because Smith produced no diff, pipeline.Run() sets WorkerFailed and returns immediately. The bead stays in_progress with no active worker or branch, requiring manual reset. This is the same class of issue as the rate-limit stuck-bead (Forge-rpc) but triggered by a different code path. Fix options: (1) no-diff should be treated as a human-attention case, not a hard failure — flag the bead with needs_human=1 and reset to open, (2) if pipeline detects no-diff on first iteration it could retry with a clarified prompt, (3) at minimum the daemon's pipeline error handler must reset the bead to open when outcome.Error is non-nil (the rate-limit fix in Forge-rpc likely covers this too — verify). Observed: 09:56:26 warden_reject [Forge-h7x] No changes detected — Smith produced no diff; bead remained in_progress.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:36Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:36Z"}

{"id":"Forge-pax","title":"Warden: default to approve (not request_changes) when verdict cannot be parsed","description":"When warden's output cannot be parsed into a structured verdict, the fallback at warden.go:299 defaults to VerdictRequestChanges with 'Could not parse structured verdict; defaulting to request_changes'. This is conservative but wasteful: it burns an iteration in the pipeline loop and spawns Smith again needlessly. Since pipeline already defaults to VerdictApprove when warden errors out entirely (warden.go review error path), the same logic should apply to parse failures. Recommendation: change the unparseable-output default from VerdictRequestChanges to VerdictApprove, log a warning, and let the PR go to human review. Observed: 10:04:33 warden_reject [Forge-pgi] Verdict: request_changes — Inferred request_changes from output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:56Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:56Z"}

old_value: {"id":"Forge-irf","title":"Add clarification_needed status for beads that require human input before starting","description":"Some beads are genuinely ambiguous and cannot be started without human clarification (e.g. 'which auth library?', 'should this replace X or wrap it?', 'URL of the external service?'). Currently these either fail silently (Smith produces no diff), stall in iteration loops, or exhaust all attempts uselessly. Proposed: (1) add a 'clarification_needed' flag or status that can be set via 'bd update \u003cid\u003e --clarification-needed' (analogous to needs_human), (2) the Needs Human Attention panel (Forge-h7x) should show these beads in a dedicated section so operators can answer and then clear the flag, (3) Forge should skip beads with clarification_needed in its poller (same as needs_human=1). For now, operators flag these manually; a future Planner worker (see Forge-yqb) may detect and set this automatically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:38:29Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T09:38:29Z"}

{"id":"Forge-zx5","title":"Hearth: Live Activity panel expands downward pushing other panels off screen","description":"As events scroll through the Live Activity sub-panel, the panel grows downward, pushing the Queue and Event log panels off the screen. Root cause suspicion: renderWorkers() returns a lipgloss.JoinVertical of two sub-panels but never wraps the result in style.Height(height), so the combined height is not bounded. Also, renderWorkerActivity maxVisible calculation uses 'height - 4' but lipgloss border padding may differ, causing a few extra lines to bleed through. Fix: ensure renderWorkers clamps its total output to contentHeight, or verify that each sub-panel's style.Height() correctly clips overflow rather than expanding the box.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:13Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T09:37:13Z"}

{"id":"Forge-70e","title":"Add Schematic pre-worker to decompose large or ambiguous beads before Smith starts","description":"Large or multi-part beads cause Smith to either (a) produce partial implementations, (b) exceed context limits, or (c) choose one interpretation of an ambiguous bead and get rejected by warden. Proposal: add a 'Schematic' pre-worker (fitting the forge metaphor: the schematic is drawn before the smith starts forging). The Schematic worker runs before Smith and: (1) reads the bead title+description, (2) spawns a lightweight AI session to analyse scope and propose either a decomposition into sub-beads OR a single focused implementation plan, (3) if decomposition is needed, creates the sub-beads via bd and marks the parent as blocked, (4) if the bead requires external clarification it sets clarification_needed and exits (see Forge-irf), (5) otherwise emits a focused implementation plan that is appended to Smith's prompt. Schematic is optional and gated by a size/complexity heuristic (e.g. description \u003e N words, or an explicit 'decompose: true' tag on the bead). The forge.yaml should allow opting out per-anvil.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:39:30Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T09:39:30Z"}

{"id":"Forge-70e","title":"Add Schematic pre-worker to decompose large or ambiguous beads before Smith starts","description":"Large or multi-part beads cause Smith to either (a) produce partial implementations, (b) exceed context limits, or (c) choose one interpretation of an ambiguous bead and get rejected by warden. Proposal: add a 'Schematic' pre-worker (fitting the forge metaphor: the schematic is drawn before the smith starts forging). The Schematic worker runs before Smith and: (1) reads the bead title+description, (2) spawns a lightweight AI session to analyse scope and propose either a decomposition into sub-beads OR a single focused implementation plan, (3) if decomposition is needed, creates the sub-beads via bd and marks the parent as blocked, (4) if the bead requires external clarification it sets clarification_needed and exits (see Forge-irf), (5) otherwise emits a focused implementation plan that is appended to Smith's prompt. Schematic is optional and gated by a size/complexity heuristic (e.g. description \u003e N words, or an explicit 'decompose: true' tag on the bead). The forge.yaml should allow opting out per-anvil.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T09:39:30Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T12:15:50Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T12:15:50Z","close_reason":"Implemented by Forge"}

{"id":"Forge-dka","title":"Bug: daemon only dispatches one smith at a time after initial batch completes","description":"With max_smiths_per_anvil=4 and max_total_smiths=7, Forge correctly starts 3 smiths initially. But after those complete, it only dispatches one smith at a time instead of filling back up to the configured limits. The poll loop or dispatch logic likely has a bug where it only picks up one bead per poll cycle instead of filling available capacity. Check internal/daemon/ dispatch logic — the loop that checks available capacity and dispatches new workers should fill all available slots each cycle, not just one.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:26:45Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:26:45Z"}

{"id":"Forge-0nv","title":"Smith prompt: add escalation instructions for needs_human flagging","description":"The Smith prompt template (internal/prompt/prompt.go) and AGENTS.md have no guidance on when a smith should signal it needs human help. Currently the only paths to needs_human are: Warden detecting no-diff, Schematic returning ActionClarify, and retry exhaustion. Smiths themselves should be instructed to explicitly flag when they encounter: ambiguous requirements they cannot resolve with reasonable interpretation, missing configuration or secrets they cannot access, external dependencies or URLs they need but do not have, architectural decisions that require human judgment. Update the default prompt template to include a section explaining how and when to escalate. The smith can signal this by exiting with a specific message pattern that the pipeline detects (e.g. NEEDS_HUMAN: reason), or by writing to a conventions file. Coordinate with how pipeline.go currently handles outcomes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:26:06Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:26:06Z"}

{"id":"Forge-30y","title":"Dispatch circuit breaker: stop retrying after N consecutive failures","description":"When a bead fails dispatch repeatedly (claimBead fails, smith crashes on startup, etc.), Forge currently retries it every poll cycle forever. Adopt gastown's circuit breaker pattern: track consecutive dispatch failures per bead. After 3 failures, mark the bead as circuit-broken (set needs_human=1 with reason) so it stops being dispatched. This prevents one poison bead from consuming capacity every 30s. Implementation: add a dispatch_failures counter to the retries table (or a new column). In dispatchBead error paths, increment the counter. In pollAndDispatch, skip beads that have hit the threshold. Reset the counter when a human retries from the Needs Attention panel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:40Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:40Z"}

{"id":"Forge-08b","title":"Warden feedback passthrough: give Smith structured context on retry","description":"When the Warden requests changes, the pipeline loops back to Smith for another iteration. Currently Smith starts fresh with just the original bead description — it does not receive the Warden's specific feedback about what was wrong. Pass the Warden's review comments as structured context in the Smith prompt for iteration 2+. The prompt builder already has access to iteration count; extend it to accept warden feedback text. This should significantly improve fix rates on retry iterations since Smith will know exactly what the Warden objected to instead of guessing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:18Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:18Z"}

old_value: {"id":"Forge-rx8","title":"Hearth: interactive actions for Needs Attention beads","description":"When a bead is selected in the Needs Attention panel, allow the operator to take actions via keyboard shortcuts or a popup menu. Default action options: (1) Retry — clear needs_human flag and reset retry count, putting bead back in queue; (2) Dismiss — mark bead as resolved/won't-fix so it leaves the panel; (3) Rebase \u0026 Retry — for collateral CI failures, trigger a rebase then retry; (4) Bump Priority — change the bead's priority; (5) View Logs — show the last smith/warden log for this bead. Actions should go through the existing IPC protocol (may need new IPC commands). Consider a small popup/overlay with the action list when pressing Enter on a selected bead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:25:56Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:25:56Z"}

{"id":"Forge-hro","title":"Cost guardrails: daily spending ceiling that pauses dispatch","description":"We track per-bead and per-day costs in bead_costs and daily_costs tables, but there is no enforcement. Add a configurable daily_cost_limit setting (in USD). When the running total for today exceeds the limit, pause dispatch until the next calendar day (or until the operator manually resumes). Log an event when the ceiling is hit. Show the current spend vs limit in forge status output. This prevents runaway costs from a bad decomposition that creates many sub-beads or from smiths that churn through retries burning tokens.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:58Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:58Z"}

{"id":"Forge-nb7","title":"Schematic: log sub-bead details in events for visibility","description":"Schematic logs schematic_started/done/skipped events but the done message only says how many sub-beads were created, not their IDs or titles. When Schematic decomposes a parent bead, log each created sub-bead as a separate event (or a single event with a JSON payload listing all sub-bead IDs and titles). This gives operators visibility into what was created without having to run bd list. Also consider emitting a notification (Teams webhook) when decomposition happens, since it changes the work queue significantly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:08Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:08Z"}

{"id":"Forge-qa8","title":"Stale worker detection: identify stuck smiths via progress heartbeats","description":"Currently Forge only kills smiths that exceed smith_timeout. There is no way to distinguish a smith that is actively working from one that is hung (e.g. waiting on a blocked API call or stuck in a loop). Adopt gastown's health-state concept: periodically check smith log file modification time or process CPU usage. If a smith shows no progress for a configurable interval (e.g. 5 minutes of no log output), mark it as stalled in the workers table. The Needs Attention panel (Forge-6x7) can then surface stalled workers. Consider adding a stalled phase or status. This is different from timeout — timeout kills the process, stale detection warns the operator before the timeout fires.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:50Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:50Z"}

{"id":"Forge-ruv","title":"Forge: detect and recover orphaned in-progress beads on startup","description":"On daemon startup (and periodically), beads with status=in_progress but no active worker and no open PR may be orphaned (interrupted mid-session). The daemon should identify these and either reset them to open (so they can be retried) or log them for operator review. Key constraints: only touch beads belonging to THIS forge config's anvils (never touch beads from other anvil configs), and never reset a bead that has an active worker or open PR.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:25:15Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:53:56Z"}

{"id":"Forge-36y","title":"Hearth Queue: split into Ready/Unlabeled/In-Progress sections with label action","description":"Split the Queue panel in Hearth into three sections (or a tabbed/grouped list):

1. **Ready** — beads returned by bd ready that have the auto_dispatch_tag label (e.g. forgeReady). These are the beads Forge will pick up.
2. **Unlabeled** — beads returned by bd ready that do NOT have the auto_dispatch_tag. These are available work but not yet approved for Forge. Only show this section when auto_dispatch is set to 'tagged' in forge.yaml.
3. **In Progress** — beads with status=in_progress, showing who/what they are assigned to (worker ID or smith PID).

When the user selects a bead in the Unlabeled section and presses Enter (or a hotkey like 'l'), add the auto_dispatch_tag label to that bead via bd update <id> --add-label <tag>. This would let operators approve beads for Forge processing directly from the TUI without switching to the CLI.

Implementation notes:
- The poller already calls bd ready --json which returns beads with their labels. Filtering by tag presence is straightforward.
- For in-progress beads, query bd list --status=in_progress --json (or use the existing activeBeads sync.Map / DB workers table).
- The label action needs a new IPC command (e.g. 'tag_bead') so Hearth can ask the daemon to run bd update.
- Consider using visual separators or section headers within the existing Queue panel rather than adding more panels.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T17:36:18Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:36:18Z"}

{"id":"Forge-36y","title":"Hearth Queue: split into Ready/Unlabeled/In-Progress sections with label action","description":"Split the Queue panel in Hearth into three sections (or a tabbed/grouped list):

1. **Ready** — beads returned by bd ready that have the auto_dispatch_tag label (e.g. forgeReady). These are the beads Forge will pick up.
2. **Unlabeled** — beads returned by bd ready that do NOT have the auto_dispatch_tag. These are available work but not yet approved for Forge. Only show this section when auto_dispatch is set to 'tagged' in forge.yaml.
3. **In Progress** — beads with status=in_progress, showing who/what they are assigned to (worker ID or smith PID).

When the user selects a bead in the Unlabeled section and presses Enter (or a hotkey like 'l'), add the auto_dispatch_tag label to that bead via bd update <id> --add-label <tag>. This would let operators approve beads for Forge processing directly from the TUI without switching to the CLI.

Implementation notes:
- The poller already calls bd ready --json which returns beads with their labels. Filtering by tag presence is straightforward.
- For in-progress beads, query bd list --status=in_progress --json (or use the existing activeBeads sync.Map / DB workers table).
- The label action needs a new IPC command (e.g. 'tag_bead') so Hearth can ask the daemon to run bd update.
- Consider using visual separators or section headers within the existing Queue panel rather than adding more panels.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T17:36:18Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:01:10Z"}

{"id":"Forge-hro","title":"Cost guardrails: daily spending ceiling that pauses dispatch","description":"We track per-bead and per-day costs in bead_costs and daily_costs tables, but there is no enforcement. Add a configurable daily_cost_limit setting (in USD). When the running total for today exceeds the limit, pause dispatch until the next calendar day (or until the operator manually resumes). Log an event when the ceiling is hit. Show the current spend vs limit in forge status output. This prevents runaway costs from a bad decomposition that creates many sub-beads or from smiths that churn through retries burning tokens.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:58Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:51Z"}

{"id":"Forge-6x7","title":"Hearth: split Queue area into Queue + Needs Attention panels","description":"Split the left-side Queue panel area into two stacked rows. Top row keeps the existing Queue (pending beads). Bottom row adds a new 'Needs Attention' panel showing beads with needs_human=1 or clarification_needed=1 from the retries table. Each entry should show: bead ID, anvil, title, and reason (exhausted retries, no-diff, clarification needed, collateral CI, rate-limited). Use NeedsHumanBeads() from state DB. The panel should follow the same styling conventions as the existing Queue panel (priority colors, scrollable list). Navigable via Tab like other panels.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:25:48Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:01:25Z"}

{"id":"Forge-6x7","title":"Hearth: split Queue area into Queue + Needs Attention panels","description":"Split the left-side Queue panel area into two stacked rows. Top row keeps the existing Queue (pending beads). Bottom row adds a new 'Needs Attention' panel showing beads with needs_human=1 or clarification_needed=1 from the retries table. Each entry should show: bead ID, anvil, title, and reason (exhausted retries, no-diff, clarification needed, collateral CI, rate-limited). Use NeedsHumanBeads() from state DB. The panel should follow the same styling conventions as the existing Queue panel (priority colors, scrollable list). Navigable via Tab like other panels.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:25:48Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:08:04Z"}

{"id":"Forge-qa8","title":"Stale worker detection: identify stuck smiths via progress heartbeats","description":"Currently Forge only kills smiths that exceed smith_timeout. There is no way to distinguish a smith that is actively working from one that is hung (e.g. waiting on a blocked API call or stuck in a loop). Adopt gastown's health-state concept: periodically check smith log file modification time or process CPU usage. If a smith shows no progress for a configurable interval (e.g. 5 minutes of no log output), mark it as stalled in the workers table. The Needs Attention panel (Forge-6x7) can then surface stalled workers. Consider adding a stalled phase or status. This is different from timeout — timeout kills the process, stale detection warns the operator before the timeout fires.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:52:50Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:01:04Z"}

{"id":"Forge-nb7","title":"Schematic: log sub-bead details in events for visibility","description":"Schematic logs schematic_started/done/skipped events but the done message only says how many sub-beads were created, not their IDs or titles. When Schematic decomposes a parent bead, log each created sub-bead as a separate event (or a single event with a JSON payload listing all sub-bead IDs and titles). This gives operators visibility into what was created without having to run bd list. Also consider emitting a notification (Teams webhook) when decomposition happens, since it changes the work queue significantly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:08Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:57Z"}

{"id":"Forge-lvk","title":"Show exhausted PRs in Needs Attention panel with retry action","description":"PRs that have exhausted their CI-fix or review-fix attempt limits (ci_fix_count >= maxCI=5 or review_fix_count >= maxRev=5) silently stop being worked by Bellows with no visibility in the TUI.

Desired behaviour:
- Such PRs should appear in the Hearth TUI Needs Attention panel (left column) with a clear reason, e.g. 'CI fix exhausted (5/5)' or 'Review fix exhausted (5/5)' or 'Conflicting rebase exhausted (3/3)'.
- The panel entry should include the PR number, bead ID, anvil, and reason.
- A Retry action in the action menu should reset the counters (ci_fix_count=0, review_fix_count=0) and set status back to open, so Bellows immediately re-detects and dispatches a new fix cycle on its next poll.
- A Dismiss action should mark the PR as closed/stale so it no longer appears.

Implementation notes:
- Add a new db query (e.g. db.ExhaustedPRs()) that returns PRs with ci_fix_count >= 5 OR review_fix_count >= 5 (or matching rebase count), with status NOT IN (merged, closed).
- Extend FetchNeedsAttention in internal/hearth/data.go to include these results, mapped to NeedsAttentionItem with Reason field set.
- Use the existing action menu infrastructure in hearth.go (ActionRetry / ActionDismiss), wiring Retry to call db.UpdatePRLifecycle(id, 0, 0, ...) + db.UpdatePRStatus(id, PROpen).
- The maxCI / maxRev thresholds are currently hardcoded in lifecycle.Manager (5/5/3). Consider exposing them via config (forge.yaml settings.max_ci_fix_attempts, settings.max_review_fix_attempts) or at minimum reading from a shared constant so the DB query threshold matches.
- The listprs and resetpr tools in cmd/ are ad-hoc helpers; this feature makes them unnecessary for normal operator use.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-06T19:39:20Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-06T19:39:20Z"}

{"id":"Forge-wfa","title":"Exhausted lifecycle caps should surface in Needs Attention","description":"When lifecycle.go exhausts CI fix, review fix, or rebase caps, it only logs EventLifecycleExhausted. It does NOT set needs_human=1 in the retries table, so the PR silently stops retrying without appearing in the Hearth Needs Attention panel. The lifecycle handler in daemon.go should call recordDispatchFailure or a new method to mark the PR's bead as needs_human with a reason like 'CI fix attempts exhausted (5/5)'. The Needs Attention panel should show these with a 'Retry' action that resets the relevant counter.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:56Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:56Z"}

{"id":"Forge-10d","title":"Add golangci-lint to Temper Go checks","description":"Temper currently runs only go build, go vet, and go test for Go projects. Add golangci-lint as an optional step (skip if binary not found). golangci-lint catches a wide range of issues: unused code, error handling, style, complexity, security patterns. Add it after vet, before test. Make it configurable per-anvil via .forge/temper.yaml or forge.yaml anvil settings so repos can opt out.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:22Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:22Z"}

{"id":"Forge-ahj","title":"Changelog fragment system (adapted from Heimdall)","description":"Forge has no changelog. Adopt Heimdall's changelog.d/ fragment pattern (English-only, no Norwegian needed). Each bead gets a changelog.d/\u003cbead-id\u003e.md fragment with 'category: Added|Changed|Fixed|...' header and markdown bullets. Add a Go-based assembly script (cmd/forge or scripts/) that collects fragments into CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased]. Smith prompt should instruct the agent to create a fragment when making user-visible changes. CI check can enforce fragment presence on PRs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:03Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:03Z"}

{"id":"Forge-88h","title":"Hearth: Workers panel scrolls items out of view permanently","description":"In the Hearth TUI Workers panel, scrolling down causes items at the top to disappear from the list and they cannot be scrolled back up to find them. Items should remain accessible when scrolling - the viewport should scroll but not lose items. Check the viewport/list rendering logic in internal/hearth/hearth.go for the Workers panel.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:44:46Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:44:46Z"}

{"id":"Forge-jyp","title":"Hearth: Tab panel switching selects Workers and Live Activity together","description":"When switching panels with Tab in the Hearth TUI, the Workers and Live Activity panels get selected/highlighted together instead of individually. Each panel should be selected alone when tabbing through them. Check the panel focus cycling logic in internal/hearth/hearth.go - likely the Tab key handler increments focus but the rendering treats two panels as one visual group.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:44:54Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:44:54Z"}

{"id":"Forge-q6o","title":"Configurable lifecycle retry caps in forge.yaml","description":"The maxCI (5), maxRev (5), and maxRebase (3) caps in lifecycle.go are hardcoded constants. They should be configurable via forge.yaml settings (e.g. settings.max_ci_fixes, settings.max_review_fixes, settings.max_rebases) so operators can tune them per deployment without code changes. Config reads in config.go, wired through daemon.go into lifecycle.New().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:48Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:48Z"}

{"id":"Forge-4g6","title":"CI check: enforce changelog fragment on PRs","description":"When Forge creates a PR (via ghpr.Create), check if a changelog.d/\u003cbead-id\u003e.md fragment exists in the diff. If the bead touches user-visible code but no fragment exists, either: 1) have Smith create one automatically as part of the pipeline, or 2) add a GitHub Actions workflow that fails PRs without fragments (like Heimdall's ci.yml changelog-check job). Option 1 is preferred since Forge controls the Smith prompt and can instruct it to always create a fragment for non-internal changes.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:42Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:42Z"}

{"id":"Forge-8rk","title":"Temper: add go race detector option for tests","description":"Temper runs 'go test -short ./...' but doesn't use -race. Data races are a common class of Go bugs. Add -race as an opt-in flag (default off since it slows tests and increases memory). Configurable via forge.yaml settings or per-anvil .forge/temper.yaml. When enabled, temper runs 'go test -race -short ./...'. The flag should be separate from the existing test step so failures are clearly attributed to race conditions.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:57Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:57Z"}

{"id":"Forge-o3t","title":"Scheduled govulncheck security scanning with bead creation","description":"Add a scheduled task (configurable interval, e.g. daily/weekly) that runs govulncheck on each registered anvil. If vulnerabilities are found, auto-create a bead with the findings and suggested remediation. Could run as a daemon goroutine alongside bellows polling, or as a separate 'forge scan' subcommand. The bead description should include the CVE IDs, affected packages, and upgrade paths. Priority based on severity (P1 for critical, P2 for high, P3 for medium).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:28Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:28Z"}

{"id":"Forge-pi0","title":"Release flow with GoReleaser and version tagging","description":"Forge has ldflags-based version injection but no release automation. Add a .goreleaser.yml config (similar to beads) and a release formula (.beads/formulas/forge-release.formula.toml) that: 1) assembles changelog fragments into CHANGELOG.md, 2) bumps version tag (semver), 3) runs goreleaser to build binaries. The forge version command already reads Version/Build from ldflags. Consider a 'forge release' subcommand or just use the formula with bd.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:10Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:10Z"}

{"id":"Forge-qmt","title":"Needs Attention: distinguish reason types with icons/labels","description":"The Needs Attention panel currently shows all items identically. Enhance it to show the reason category: dispatch exhausted (circuit breaker), CI fix exhausted, review fix exhausted, rebase exhausted, clarification needed. Each category should have a distinct icon or label prefix so operators can quickly triage. This depends on Forge-wfa (exhausted lifecycle caps surfacing in Needs Attention) being implemented first.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:35:04Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:35:04Z"}

{"id":"Forge-9do","title":"Hearth: Enter actions menu for Unlabeled queue items with label option","description":"In the Queue panel, when an Unlabeled bead is selected, pressing Enter should open an actions menu (similar to the Needs Attention actions menu). The menu should include an option to apply the auto-dispatch label (same as pressing 'l'). Currently Enter/actions only works in the Needs Attention panel. Extend the action menu system in internal/hearth/hearth.go to also work for Queue items in the 'unlabeled' section, with at minimum a 'Label for dispatch' action that calls OnTagBead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T12:18:59Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T12:18:59Z"}

{"id":"Forge-m97","title":"Smith: instruct workers to create beads for unrelated issues found during work","description":"When a Smith worker encounters preexisting bugs, code quality issues, unrelated errors, or other problems outside the scope of its assigned bead, it should create new beads for them rather than ignoring them or trying to fix them inline. Update the Smith prompt (internal/prompt/) to instruct the agent: if you discover issues unrelated to your current task, run 'bd create' with an appropriate title, description, type, and priority. The worker should NOT attempt to fix unrelated issues itself — just log them as new beads for future work. This turns every Smith run into a passive code audit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T12:19:47Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T12:19:47Z"}

{"id":"Forge-qvr","title":"Hearth: Needs Attention item stays visible after Retry action","description":"When pressing Retry on a bead in the Needs Attention panel, the work starts (a new worker appears), but the bead remains in the Needs Attention list. It should disappear immediately after a successful retry. The retry IPC handler in daemon.go resets the retry record (clears needs_human), but the Hearth TUI doesn't refresh the Needs Attention panel after the action completes. Either the retry handler should trigger a FetchNeedsAttention refresh, or the executeAction method in hearth.go should request a data refresh after a successful retry/dismiss action.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T12:23:47Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T12:23:47Z"}

{"id":"Forge-hhx","title":"Stale worker detection should exclude bellows/lifecycle workers","description":"The stale worker detection (heartbeat monitoring from Forge-qa8) flags bellows and other long-running background workers as stuck because they don't produce frequent log activity like Smith workers do. Bellows is a polling loop that only writes logs when PR state changes — it can be silent for long stretches and that's normal. The watchdog/stale detection logic should either: 1) exclude workers with specific phases (e.g. 'bellows', 'lifecycle') from staleness checks, or 2) use a different heartbeat mechanism for long-running monitors vs short-lived Smith/cifix/reviewfix workers. Check internal/watchdog/ and the stale worker detection code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T12:30:03Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T12:30:03Z"}

{"id":"Forge-npl","title":"Hearth: Ready to Merge panel with merge action","description":"Add a new panel to the Hearth TUI that lists PRs ready to merge. A PR is ready when: CI checks pass, no unresolved review comments, no merge conflicts, and has approval. The panel should be reactive — PRs must disappear immediately when: new review comments arrive, another PR merge causes conflicts, CI fails, or approval is dismissed. Data source: bellows already tracks all this state (CIPassing, HasApproval, NeedsChanges, HasUnresolvedThreads, IsConflicting) in prSnapshot. Add a DB query or bellows method to surface PRs where all conditions are met. The panel needs Enter/actions support with a 'Merge' option that calls 'gh pr merge --squash' (or configurable merge strategy) via IPC command. After merge, bellows will detect the EventPRMerged and lifecycle will close the bead automatically. {"id":"Forge-hco","title":"Hearth: move Events panel into right column below Live Activity","description":"Currently the Events panel is a full-width bottom strip. {"id":"Forge-fox","title":"Warden: learnable review rules from Copilot feedback","description":"The Warden currently reviews with a generic 5-point checklist while Copilot catches many specific patterns (data races, width inconsistencies, error swallowing, etc). Build a learning system so the Warden accumulates domain-specific review rules over time.

## Design

### 1. Warden Rules File
Per-anvil file at `.forge/warden-rules.yaml` containing learned review patterns:

```yaml
rules:
  - id: race-ctx-field
    category: concurrency
    pattern: "Plain struct field accessed from multiple goroutines"
    check: "Verify context/cancel fields use atomic.Value or are set before goroutines start"
    source: "copilot:PR#65"
    added: 2026-03-07
  - id: panel-width-consistency
    category: ui
    pattern: "Panel width calculations in TUI code"
    check: "Ensure all panels in the same row use the same width accounting formula"
    source: "copilot:PR#65"
    added: 2026-03-07
```

### 2. Warden Prompt Integration
In `buildReviewPrompt()` (internal/warden/warden.go), read `.forge/warden-rules.yaml` from the anvil path and append the rules as a numbered checklist section in the prompt. Keep it concise — each rule becomes one line like: "[ ] Check: {check} (pattern: {pattern})".

### 3. Learning Pipeline — `forge warden learn`
New subcommand that:
1. Fetches unresolved Copilot review comments from recent/specified PRs via GitHub API
2. Groups and deduplicates similar comments
3. Uses Claude to distill each unique pattern into a rule (category, pattern, check)
4. Appends to `.forge/warden-rules.yaml` with provenance (source PR)
5. Optionally runs in `--dry-run` mode to preview rules before adding

Could also run automatically: after a PR is merged, bellows triggers a learn pass on any Copilot comments from that PR.

### 4. Rule Lifecycle
- Rules accumulate in the YAML file (committed to the repo so all Forge instances share them)
- Manual pruning via editing the file or `forge warden forget <rule-id>`
- Rules that trigger false positives can be removed or refined
- Over time the Warden becomes specialized for each repo's patterns

### Key Files
- `internal/warden/warden.go` — `buildReviewPrompt()` reads rules file
- `cmd/forge/warden.go` — new `forge warden learn` subcommand
- `.forge/warden-rules.yaml` — per-anvil learned rules (committed)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:05:48Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:05:48Z"}

Add a pr_merge_failed event to the daemon log on merge failure, and ensure the TUI shows the error prominently in the status bar. Found when --yes flag was unsupported by gh CLI and merge silently failed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:11:51Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:11:51Z"}

{"id":"Forge-lx3","title":"Bug: CI fix worker completion doesn't reset lifecycle CIPassing flag — PR gets permanently stuck","description":"After a CI fix worker (ActionFixCI) completes in daemon.go, there is no NotifyCIFixCompleted() call on the lifecycle manager. The lifecycle's st.CIPassing stays false and st.CINeedsFix stays true. When bellows next polls and re-detects CI failure (via its own reset snapshot), the lifecycle guard at line 267 (`if !st.CIPassing`) triggers 'already failing, skipping dispatch' and drops the event. The PR is permanently stuck — no new cifix workers are ever dispatched.

Compare with ActionFixReview (line 528) which correctly calls NotifyReviewFixCompleted() to reset ReviewNeedsFix.

## Fix
1. Add NotifyCIFixCompleted() to lifecycle Manager that resets st.CIPassing=true and st.CINeedsFix=false (mirror NotifyReviewFixCompleted logic)
2. Call it from daemon.go after the cifix worker completes (line ~491), gated on res.Error==nil
3. Also handle the DB status update: if CIPassing is reset and no ReviewNeedsFix, transition needs_fix → open so bellows can re-detect cleanly

## Reproduction
PR #76 (Forge-8rk) hit this exact bug: after retry_reset at 17:28, rebase succeeded, cifix worker ran at 17:32 and completed at 17:33, but all subsequent ci_failed events from bellows were dropped by lifecycle. PR stuck in needs_fix with ci_fix_count=1 forever.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:53:38Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:53:38Z"}

{"id":"Forge-1bq","title":"Warden learn: semantic comment grouping instead of exact-match","description":"GroupComments() in learn.go only groups comments with identical normalized body text. Copilot comments about the same pattern but with different wording (e.g. 'missing error check on Open()' vs 'error from ReadFile not handled') create separate rules instead of being grouped. Consider using Claude to cluster semantically similar comments before distillation, or use a simpler heuristic like keyword overlap scoring.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:41:35Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:41:35Z"}

{"id":"Forge-y6m","title":"Bug: merged PR lingers in Ready to Merge panel until next bellows poll (up to 2 min)","description":"After a successful merge via the Ready to Merge action, the daemon (daemon.go:1699-1704) only logs an event and returns 'ok' to the TUI. It does NOT:

1. Update the PR status in the DB to 'merged' — so ReadyToMergePRs() still returns it
2. Call bellows.Refresh() — so bellows won't re-poll until the next 2-minute interval
3. Update lifecycle state — so lifecycle still considers it active
4. Close the bead or complete workers

The PR stays visible in the Ready to Merge panel until bellows happens to poll GitHub, detects state=MERGED, and updates the DB. With the default 2-minute bellows interval, this means the PR sits there for up to 2 minutes after being merged.

## Fix
After a successful ghpr.Merge() call (line 1698), add:
1. `d.db.UpdatePRStatus(pr.ID, state.PRMerged)` — immediately remove from ReadyToMergePRs query
2. `d.bellowsMonitor.Refresh()` — trigger immediate bellows poll to handle downstream effects (bead close, worker cleanup)
3. Optionally emit a lifecycle EventPRMerged so lifecycle runs ActionCloseBead

The DB update alone would give instant feedback in the TUI. The bellows refresh ensures all downstream cleanup happens promptly rather than waiting for the next poll cycle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T17:00:10Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T17:00:10Z"}

{"id":"Forge-kna","title":"Temper: LoadAnvilConfig swallows non-ENOENT errors and prints to stderr","description":"LoadAnvilConfig in temper.go returns nil for any read/unmarshal error, but docs say it returns nil only when the file doesn't exist. Permission errors, corrupt YAML, etc. are silently swallowed. It also prints errors to stderr via fmt.Fprintf instead of returning them, which is bad for daemon flows that need structured error handling.\n\nFix: return the error for non-os.IsNotExist cases so callers (loadAnvilTemperCached in daemon.go) can handle/log them properly. Also fix loadAnvilTemperCached to handle non-ENOENT os.Stat errors without spamming logs on every dispatch.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no","created_at":"2026-03-07T17:08:40Z","created_by":"Robin Smith","updated_at":"2026-03-07T17:08:40Z"}

No code changes needed.
